prompt: Your task is to generate the **official README.md** for Aetheris. This is NOT a normal GitHub README. This should be one of the best open-source README files ever written. It should feel like the documentation quality of Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, LangChain, React, Next.js and Linux combined. The README must completely explain Aetheris to someone who has never heard of it. Never summarize. Never write placeholder text. Never leave TODOs. Everything must be production-ready. Everything must be beautifully formatted. Everything must be visually appealing. Use Mermaid diagrams. Use tables. Use callouts. Use emojis only where appropriate. Write in professional technical English. The README should be approximately 8,000-15,000 words. VERY IMPORTANT: The README MUST start with installation. Do NOT explain Aetheris first. Users should immediately know how to install it. Installation must feel as simple as Docker or Node. Example: pip install aetheris, aetheris --version, aetheris init, aetheris start. DO NOT explain internal installation steps. DO NOT explain cloning repository first. DO NOT explain Python virtual environments. DO NOT explain dependencies. Assume installation is one command. Installation should look effortless. README ORDER: Generate sections in EXACTLY this order: 1) Hero Section 2) Installation 3) Quick Start 4) What is Aetheris? 5) Why Aetheris? 6) Core Philosophy 7) What Can Aetheris Build? 8) Features 9) AntiGravity 10) Repository Intelligence 11) Engineering Lifecycle 12) Architecture 13) CLI 14) Dashboard 15) Skills 16) Departments 17) RFC Documentation 18) SPEC Documentation 19) Project Workflow 20) Folder Structure 21) Configuration 22) Examples 23) Benchmarks 24) Best Practices 25) Plugin Development 26) Enterprise 27) Roadmap 28) Community 29) License. The README must look like it belongs to a billion-dollar open-source engineering platform. Never write generic marketing. Explain everything deeply. Use diagrams. Use architecture drawings. Use beautiful tables. Use examples. Use command outputs. Use code blocks. Use workflows. Use engineering terminology. Every section should be comprehensive enough that a new engineer can understand Aetheris without reading any other document. The final README should feel like the homepage, installation guide, user manual, developer guide, architecture overview, and product documentation combined into one world-class document.
